title: Wrong CSS interpretation on fresh tab
author: jjtmp

title: Build with Debian: Qt5 workaround, Qt6 fail
author: jjtmp
body:
Here on my Debian there are some problems with Qt5 (`-DENABLE_QT5=ON`) and Qt6 (default):

1. There's no Qml (-dev) package in my package manager. Also CMake doesn't find it. However the Qt5 build succeeds and the program opens my website fine when I just remove Qml requirement from `find_package()` in CMakeLists.txt. No change is required for linker.
2. There's no NetworkPrivate (-dev) package in my package manager. I just removed it from CMakeLists.txt but I cannot tell whether it compiles. I also want to mention that linking requires Qt::NetworkPrivate in CMakeLists.txt but you would probably mean **Qt6**::NetworkPrivate? Side node: there _is_ /usr/lib/i386-linux-gnu/qt6/mkspecs/modules/qt_lib_network_private.pri which comes from qt6-base-dev package
3. QtWebEnginePage.h:27 includes <QtWebEngineWidgets/QWebEngineCertificateError> but Qt6 dev doesn't have this file. (This is where my build session ends for now.) For reference:
   ```
   $ find /usr/include -name  QWebEngineCertificateError 
   /usr/include/i386-linux-gnu/qt5/QtWebEngineWidgets/QWebEngineCertificateError
   /usr/include/i386-linux-gnu/qt6/QtWebEngineCore/QWebEngineCertificateError
   $ 
   ```

I cannot tell more whether Qt6 build works on Debian as I just came so far.


Side remark: in CMakeLists CPACK_DEBIAN_PACKAGE_DEPENDS has just `libqt5webkit5 (>=5.212.0~alpha2)`. I would rather make it choosable: `libqt5webkit5 (>=5.212.0~alpha2) | libqt5webengine5`. Shall I open a pull request for this?

title: Userscripts engine is not working on stable version on Rolling Release systems (Garuda Linux)
author: gleyconJ
body:
Hello, Otter Browser team.

I am opening this issue to report that the native userscripts feature appears to be non-functional on the stable version of the browser when running on a modern rolling-release operating system.

### Problem Description

User scripts can be added normally through the `Tools > Addons` panel, and they persist in the list after restarting the browser. However, they produce absolutely no effect on webpages. The functionality seems to be completely disabled in practice, although the management interface works.

### System Environment

* **Operating System:** Garuda Linux (Arch-based, rolling release, fully updated as of September 2025)
* **Desktop Environment:** KDE Plasma 6
* **Otter Version:** 1.0.03 (the latest stable version available in the official repositories)

### Troubleshooting Steps Taken

Before opening this issue, I performed a series of tests to isolate the cause of the problem:

1.  **Tested with Multiple Scripts:** I tried several scripts from different sources (Greasy Fork and custom-written scripts), including:
    * Global dark mode scripts (using both `filter: invert` and `GM_addStyle`).
    * Site-specific scripts (e.g., a dark theme for Wikipedia).
    * A minimal, custom-written test script that only changed the background and text color, to ensure there were no errors in the code.
2.  **Technically Corrected Scripts:** Scripts that used less robust CSS injection methods (`createElement('style')`) were rewritten to use `GM_addStyle`, but they still failed to work.
3.  **File Management:** I confirmed that the `.user.js` files are saved correctly in the `~/.config/otter/profiles/default/userscripts/` directory.

None of the tested scripts had any visual or functional effect.

### Hypothesis

My suspicion is that an incompatibility exists between the stable version of Otter (from 2022) and the newer libraries of a rolling-release system like Garuda (Qt versions, etc., from 2025). It is likely that this incompatibility has silently broken the userscripts execution engine.

Could you please confirm if this is expected behavior or if there are any other debugging steps I could try?

Thank you for your excellent work on this browser!

title: Outdated WebKit Engine
author: jabcreations
body:
Hi,

I'm the developer of a web platform and after adding asynchronous functions I've had to reduce browser support to Chrome 55+, Firefox 52+ and Safari 10.1+. Unfortunately Otter uses what is effectively Safari 10.0 and just barely is no longer supported by my platform.

I really liked having a WebKit (not Blink!) based browser to test with but it was already slow and fairly dated when I first discovered it. Now it's the equivalent of a 3GB VRAM GPU, it'll work sometimes but pretty much every website I visit with it including this one is broken.

Offhand, I don't know how much of a PITA it would be to implement a newer version of WebKit but it seems pointless to work on the browser frame if the engine is just too dated to use. If you update please stick to WebKit, the world does not need yet another Blink based browser (e.g. Chrome Clones).

title: A built-in DHT based search engine
author: sjehuda
body:
Greetings!

I hope that this idea would find you well.

I propose to add a built-in Kademlia DHT based search engine to Otter Browser.

Just like aMule, BitMagnet, eMule, gtk-gnutella, LimeWire, Magnetico, Shareaza etc. but for links.

The technology is already available and has successfully proven itself.

See also
- https://github.com/searxng/searxng/discussions/3021
- https://lists.mailman3.com/hyperkitty/list/dillo-dev@mailman3.com/thread/CMTUY6Y37REKNKRIG5WI4AQSIZPFMDBJ/

Schimon

title: css3 test makes page hang
author: StevanWhite
body:
This very nice on-line CSS3 test doesn't do well on Otter 1.0.03 (on Manjaro Linux)

`https://css3test.com/`

1) After the page (at least partially) loads, the tab becomes unresponsive, and scrolling stops working.  Closing the tab from the File menu then causes the browser to crash.

2) The page reports 48% support for CSS3.  (As the page says, this is just the portion of CSS the browser recognizes, not what it correctly implements.)  Gecko is at 89%, and Blink at 90%.

title: MP4 in <video> tags
author: StevanWhite
body:
On Linux Mint (Otter 1.081-dev), Manjaro Linux (Otter 1.0,03), and on Haiku  (Otter 1.0,03), 
a lot of problems loading and playing MP4 movies within a `<video>` tag.

All the OSs are up to date.

This page has several different small MP4's, and works OK on modern Gecko and WebKit based browsers:

[Things that move in deep space](https://www.zipcon.net/~swhite/docs/astronomy/motion_in_deep_space.html)

The Otter on Manjaro Linux just hangs the whole window until all the movies load.
The others take a very long time to load the movies, and then do odd things in the mean-time.  I guess it's threading issues.

Cheers!

title: Support Gemini
author: sjehuda
body:
### Gemini in 100 words

> Gemini is a new internet technology supporting an electronic library of interconnected text documents. That's not a new idea, but it's not old fashioned either. It's timeless, and deserves tools which treat it as a first class concept, not a vestigial corner case. Gemini isn't about innovation or disruption, it's about providing some respite for those who feel the internet has been disrupted enough already. We're not out to change the world or destroy other technologies. We are out to build a lightweight online space where documents are just documents, in the interests of every reader's privacy, attention and bandwidth.

gemini://geminiprotocol.net

https://geminiprotocol.net

title: Bookmarks: Export to XBEL
author: sjehuda
body:
Bookmarks are store as XBEL at `~/.config/otter/bookmarks.xbel`, and it would be good to have a graphical interface to export (i.e. copy) it to a desired location.

See gemini://woodpeckersnest.space/~schapps/journal/2025-01-08-deprecate-netscape-bookmarks.gmi

title: Bookmarks: Utilize tags
author: sjehuda
body:
This is a concern which is referred to other browsers as well, yet it is independent and does not rely on other to implement it, because most of the bookmark management services primarily utilize tags.

I have wrote an article at gemini://woodpeckersnest.space/~schapps/journal/2025-01-08-deprecate-netscape-bookmarks.gmi

I that article, I sugges t to utilize only a couple or three folders.

For Otter Browser it could be:

- Bookmarks
- Speed Dial
- Trash

All the rest of the bookmarks be managed with tags.

---

I am aware that it might be less efficient to do with XBEL, in comparison to an SQLite database.

I ma willing to assist in the design of an SQLite database for this task, if XBEL is not desired when tags are implemented.
